highlights: One of Hampi's most loved restaurants

highlights: Good for falafel, hummus, and quick plates

restaurants.ambience: Hole in the wall, no frills place
restaurants.area: Very close to the Virupaksha Temple

attractions.name: Lotus Mahal
attractions.description: Elegant, lotus-shaped pavilion blending Indo-Islamic design elements. A serene and photogenic landmark, it once served as a space for royal gatherings
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Elephant Stables
attractions.description: A long row of domed chambers built for the royal elephants — grand, sturdy, and beautifully preserved
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Queen's Bath
attractions.description: A seemingly simple exterior opens into a beautifully detailed bathing chamber with arched corridors and an enormous central pool
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Matanga Hill
attractions.description: One of Hampi's highest points for a spectacular panoramic sunset over scattered ruins, boulder hills, and the Tungabhadra valley
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Virupaksha Temple
attractions.description: Hampi's most active and oldest temple, dedicated to Lord Shiva. Walk through the towering gopuram, witness ongoing rituals, and take in the carvings that span centuries of devotion
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Lakshmi Narasimha
attractions.description: Iconic monolithic statue
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Krishna Temple
attractions.description: Built by Krishnadevaraya to commemorate his victory in Odisha, the temple complex features ornate carvings and a lively bazaar street leading up to it
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Badavilinga
attractions.description: A massive monolithic Shiva Linga partially submerged in water — simple yet powerful in presence
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Hampi Archaeological Museum
attractions.description: A beautifully curated museum showcasing sculptures, coins, tools, and artefacts excavated from the Vijayanagara Empire. The gallery helps contextualise the ruins you've been exploring with detailed models and historical narratives
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Hazara Rama Temple
attractions.description: Dedicated to Lord Rama, this 15th-century temple is famed for its intricate bas-reliefs depicting scenes from the Ramayana. Every wall narrates a different episode, making it one of Hampi's most artistically rich monuments
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Palace of Vira Harihara
attractions.description: Although only foundation outlines remain today, the palace complex offers a glimpse into the grand scale and regal lifestyle of the Vijayanagara rulers, complete with sculpted details that survived centuries
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Underground Shiva Temple
attractions.description: Also known as the Prasanna Virupaksha Temple, this unique structure sits partially submerged below ground level. The cool, silent corridors give it an atmospheric, almost mystical feel
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Stepped Tank (Pushkarni)
attractions.description: Geometric architectural marvel
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Royal Center of Hampi
attractions.description: Includes the Lotus Mahal, the Elephant Stable, and the Queen's Bath. Learn about the grandeur and opulence of the Vijayanagara Empire
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

attractions.name: Sacred Center of Hampi
attractions.description: Find Narasimha Shrine, the Krishna Temple, and the Badavilinga
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

attractions.name: Tungabhadra River
attractions.description: Enjoy a coracle ride on the river
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

attractions.name: Hemkuta Hill
attractions.description: Breathtaking panoramic view of the ruins and stunning sunset
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

attractions.name: Hampi Bazaar
attractions.description: Vibrant stalls offering local textiles, stone carvings, brassware, and quirky souvenirs. Great for light snacks and people-watching
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Vijaya Vittala Temple
attractions.description: A masterpiece of architecture known for its famous Stone Chariot. Intricate carvings and serene atmosphere
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

attractions.name: Archaeological Museum
attractions.description: Museum with archaeological artifacts
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

attractions.name: Anegundi
attractions.description: Charming village with ancient temples and beautiful landscapes. Experience local culture
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide.pdf

attractions.name: Hazara Raama Temple
attractions.description: 15th-century Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Rama, known for its intricate carvings depicting scenes from the Ramayana
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf

attractions.name: Zanana Enclosure
attractions.description: Fortified area within the Vijayanagara Empire, specifically designed for the residence and leisure of royal women
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf

attractions.name: Underground Shiva Temple (Prasanna Virupaksha Temple)
attractions.description: 14th-century Hindu temple built below ground level, discovered in the 1980s after being buried for over four hundred years
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf

attractions.name: Stepped Tank
attractions.description: An architectural masterpiece of geometric precision, the tank was used for ceremonial purposes. Its perfectly symmetrical design makes it one of the most striking structures in the Royal Enclosure
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Yantrodharaka Hanuman Temple
attractions.description: 15th-century temple dedicated to Lord Hanuman, located on the banks of the Tungabhadra River, featuring Lord Hanuman in a meditative pose within a sacred Yantra
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf

attractions.name: Monolithic Bull (Nandi)
attractions.description: Colossal sculpture of Nandi carved from a single granite boulder, located at the eastern end of the Virupaksha Bazaar
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf

attractions.name: Shri Achyutaraaya Swamy Temple
attractions.description: 16th-century architectural marvel dedicated to Lord Tiruvengalanatha, showcasing intricate carvings, sculpted pillars, and ornate details
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf

attractions.name: Sanapur Lake
attractions.description: Picturesque lake that is part of the larger Tungabhadra River basin
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Hampi - All Inclusive Guide(1).pdf

attractions.name: Narasimha Shrine (Lakshmi Narasimha Temple)
attractions.description: Famous for the striking monolith of Narasimha seated on the coils of Adishesha. The scale and expressive detailing make this one of Hampi's most photographed monuments
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Coracle Ride on the Tungabhadra River
attractions.description: A gentle, circular boat ride that lets you drift along boulder-strewn riverbanks and ancient ghats. A relaxing way to appreciate Hampi's surreal landscape from the water
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

attractions.name: Achyutaraya Temple
attractions.description: A stunning 16th-century temple surrounded by boulder hills and ancient bazaar streets. The towering gopurams, intricate carvings, and quiet courtyards give you an immersive sense of Hampi's grandeur during the Vijayanagara era
source_files: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Oishee_All Inclusive Guide_Hampi.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Wear comfortable walking shoes — ruins involve uneven terrain
safety_tips._source_file: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Carry a hat, sunglasses, and sunscreen; Hampi is warm and open
safety_tips._source_file: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf
Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Start early to enjoy monuments in pleasant morning light
safety_tips._source_file: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf

safety_tips.tip: ATMs are limited around Hampi; carry some cash for entrances and small shops
safety_tips._source_file: Karnataka, Kerala and Tamil Nadu/Sandhya_All Inclusive Guide- Hampi.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Photography is allowed in most places, but flash is restricted in some temples
